COOKING_INSTRUCTIONS_FRIED: Soak your dry, clean catfish nuggets in the buttermilk and egg mixture for 15 - 30 minutes. This tenderizes the fish and creates a sticky surface for the batter to cling to. In a wide, shallow bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper, and cayenne. Fill a heavy-bottomed skillet, cast-iron pan, or Dutch oven with about 1.5 - 2 inches of oil. Heat it to 350 degrees. Working in batches, remove a few nuggets from the milk, letting the excess drip off. Toss them in the cornmeal-flour mixture until they are completely coated and shake off the excess. Carefully drop the nuggets into the hot oil (do not overcrowd the pan). Fry for about 5 -7 minutes, flipping halfway through, until they are deep golden brown and begin to float.

NAME: Shredded Hashbrowns
INGREDIENTS: Potatoes, Dextrose, Disodium Dihydrogen Pyrophosphate (To maintain natural color).
COOKING_INSTRUCTIONS_BAKED: GRILLING - Preheat Temp 375 - 400 degrees; Place item on a preheated, oiled grill. Pour 1/8 C of vegetable oil on the top of the potatoes. Allow potatoes to cook, undisturbed, until bottom shreds are golden brown (3-6 minutes). Flip potatoes and fry for an additional 4-5 minutes.Make sure product reaches a temperature of 165 degrees for 2 minutes.
COOKING_INSTRUCTIONS_FRIED: Let them thaw completely. Place the shredded potatoes in a clean kitchen towel and squeeze out as much excess liquid as possible. Spread them out on a paper towel for 10 minutes to finish air-drying. Preheat a large, heavy nonstick or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add the oil and butter. Once the butter is melted and foaming, you are ready to cook. Add the shredded hash browns, pressing them down lightly into a thin, even layer about an inch and a halk thick. Do not press them into a heavy, impenetrable block, but gently pack the edges to hold the shape. Season with salt and pepper. Leave the potatoes completely alone for 5 - 6 minutes so they can develop a deep, golden crust.

MICROWAVE - high power for 1 minute and 15 seconds.

NAME: Beef Tamales
INGREDIENTS: Contains: SOY, WHEAT, water, beef broth, yello corn meal, salt, cooked beef, enriched wheat flour (wheat flour, barley, malt, niacin, iron, thiamine mononitrate, riboflavin, folic acid), water, rye flour, yeast, salt, contains 2% or less of the following: Soybean Oil, Wheat Gluten, Dough Conditioners (Monocalcium Phosphate, Calcium Phosphate, Ammonium Sulfate), Calcium Propionate, Potassium Sorbate (Preservatives), Garlic Powder, Onion Powder, Paprika, and Salt.
COOKING_INSTRUCTIONS_BAKED: Stovetop steaming - Fill a large pot with a couple of inches of water and place a steamer basket or rack inside, stand the tamales upright in the steamer, with the open side facing up. Pack them closely together to help them stay upright, bring the water to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cover the pot and steam the tamales for 60 - 75 minutes. To check for doneness, remove one tamale, the husk should peel away cleanly from the masa. If it sticks, steam for an additional 10 - 15 minutes. Oven - Preheat oven to 350 degrees, place tamales in a baking dish, cover with foil, and add a bit of water to the bottom of the dish to create steam, bake for about 30 - 40 minutes, check occasionally to ensure they don't dry out.
COOKING_INSTRUCTIONS_FRIED: No Frying Instructions
